name: Lutetium
uses: Lutetium is little used outside research. One of its few commercial uses is as a catalyst for cracking hydrocarbons in oil refineries.
naturalAbundance: In common with many other lanthanides, the main source of lutetium is the mineral monazite. It is extracted, with difficulty, by reducing the anhydrous fluoride with calcium metal.
biologicalRole: Lutetium has no known biological role. It has low toxicity.
